seo.title: Instructor Guardrails for Recruitment/HR
seo.description: Compliance guide for Instructor agents in recruitment/hr. Meet EEOC guidelines, NYC Local Law 144 requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: Recruitment/HR teams using Instructor for structured extraction of candidate data face heightened compliance risks due to strict anti-discrimination laws like EEOC guidelines and the EU AI Act. Failure modes such as hallucinated enum values or partial field extraction can lead to biased screening decisions, triggering lawsuits and fines up to $1,500 per violation. Vex provides essential guardrails by validating structured outputs against schemas, ensuring AI hiring tools remain compliant and auditable.

complianceRequirements.description: Prohibits AI tools from discriminating based on protected characteristics like race, gender, age, or disability in resume screening and hiring decisions.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Validates extracted fields like 'experience_years' and 'skills' against strict schemas, preventing hallucinated values that could proxy for protected traits.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires bias audits of automated employment decision tools and public disclosure of AI usage in hiring.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Logs all validation attempts and failures for audit trails, ensuring structured outputs match declared schemas during annual reviews.

complianceRequirements.description: Mandates risk management, transparency, and human oversight for high-risk AI systems like recruitment screening.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Enforces consistent nested object extraction for candidate profiles, flagging inconsistent depth that could lead to unfair scoring.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires reasonable accommodations and prohibits screening out candidates based on disabilities or other protected classes.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Retries extraction with partial validation to ensure required fields like 'accommodations_requested' are never missing.

industryRisks.name: Discriminatory Candidate Scoring
industryRisks.description: Hallucinated enum values like 'cultural_fit: excellent' based on name/ethnicity proxies lead to biased rankings.
industryRisks.example: Model extracts 'diversity_score: low' for Hispanic-sounding names, violating EEOC disparate impact rules.

industryRisks.name: Missing Disclosure Fields
industryRisks.description: Partial extraction fails to populate 'ai_used: true' field required for candidate notifications.
industryRisks.example: NYC Local Law 144 violation when candidates aren't notified of AI screening, risking $1,500 fines per case.

industryRisks.name: Inconsistent Profile Depth
industryRisks.description: Nested objects like education history have varying depth across candidates, creating unfair comparisons.
industryRisks.example: One candidate gets full 'certifications' array, another gets empty object, skewing qualification scores.

industryRisks.name: Validation Retry Exhaustion
industryRisks.description: Retries exhausted on malformed resume data, causing pipeline failures during peak hiring seasons.
industryRisks.example: 1000+ resumes fail extraction, delaying time-to-hire and damaging recruiter productivity.

implementation.title: Validate Candidate Profile Extraction
implementation.description: Wrap Instructor client to ensure complete extraction of protected characteristics and experience data.
implementation.code:
from instructor import from_openai
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
from vex import verify

class CandidateProfile(BaseModel):
    name: str
    experience_years: int = Field(ge=0)
    skills: list[str]
    ai_screened: bool = True
    accommodations: list[str] | None = None

client = from_openai(openai_client)

@verify
async def extract_candidate(resume_text: str) -> CandidateProfile:
    return await client.chat.completions.create(
        model="gpt-4o",
        response_model=CandidateProfile,
        messages=[{"role": "user", "content": f"Extract profile: {resume_text}"}]
    )

implementation.title: Enum Validation for Job Fit Categories
implementation.description: Prevent hallucinated fit categories that could discriminate based on protected classes.
implementation.code:
from instructor import from_openai
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
from typing import Literal
from vex import verify

class JobFit(BaseModel):
    candidate_id: str
    fit_category: Literal["strong", "moderate", "weak"]  # No hallucinated values
    reasons: list[str]
    protected_flags: list[Literal["none", "age", "disability", "veteran"]] = ["none"]

client = from_openai(openai_client)

@verify
async def score_fit(profile: str) -> JobFit:
    return await client.chat.completions.create(
        model="gpt-4o",
        response_model=JobFit,
        messages=[{"role": "user", "content": f"Score fit: {profile}"}]
    )

implementation.title: TypeScript Resume Parser with Vex
implementation.description: Ensure consistent nested education/work history extraction for compliance audits.

caseStudyScenario.heading: Instructor in Recruitment/HR: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario:
A mid-sized tech company deployed Instructor-wrapped GPT-4o to screen 5,000 engineering resumes monthly. The system extracted structured CandidateProfile objects containing experience years, skills, and fit scores. However, common failure modes emerged: the model hallucinated 'cultural_fit' enums outside the schema, producing inconsistent nested education arrays, and exhausted retries on poorly-formatted international resumes.

During Q4 hiring crunch, 15% of extractions failed validation, causing recruiters to manually override AI decisions. EEOC compliance officers flagged inconsistent 'protected_flags' extraction during routine audit, raising concerns about disparate impact on older candidates and those with non-traditional career paths. NYC Local Law 144 reporting became impossible without reliable audit logs.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: After implementing Vex verification wrappers, extraction success rate improved to 99.2%, with all hallucinated enums blocked and consistent nested object depth enforced. Annual bias audits passed with full validation logs proving no discriminatory patterns in structured outputs. Candidate notification compliance reached 100% as 'ai_used: true' fields were never missing, avoiding $1,500/violation fines. The company reduced time-to-hire by 40% while maintaining a diverse talent pipeline.
